input.title: Digital Core Banking System
input.description: A comprehensive digital banking application designed for retail and corporate customers. It allows users to manage multiple accounts, initiate domestic and international transfers, manage physical and virtual cards, apply for loans, and interact with customer support. The system incorporates robust KYC (Know Your Customer) workflows, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), and real-time Anti-Money Laundering (AML) monitoring via an Admin Dashboard.

user_classes.Retail Customer: An individual user accessing personal checking, savings, and loan accounts.
user_classes.Corporate Customer: A business user requiring batch payments, payroll execution, and multi-user delegation.
user_classes.Bank Admin: A technical or operational administrator handling overarching platform settings, user limits, and support escalations.
user_classes.Compliance Officer: A specialized admin role responsible for verifying KYC documents, analyzing flagged AML transactions, and locking high-risk accounts.

definitions.KYC: Know Your Customer — the mandatory process of identifying and verifying the client's identity.
definitions.AML: Anti-Money Laundering — regulations and systems deployed to prevent illicit fund movement.
definitions.ACH: Automated Clearing House — network used for electronic transfers.
definitions.SWIFT: Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication — network for international wires.

scope: User onboarding with automated biometric and document verification via third-party API
Multi-factor authentication (SMS, Email OTP, Authenticator app)
Internal, domestic, and international fund transfers
Comprehensive card management (freeze, unfreeze, block, set limits, request virtual card)
Full transaction history with advanced filtering and PDF/CSV statement generation
Compliance dashboard for officers to view and intervene in flagged transactions
out_of_scope: Stock market trading and brokerage accounts
Physical ATM deployment integrations (only ISO 8583 interfaces are assumed existing)
constraints: All backend services must be written in Java (Spring Boot) and adhere to microservice architecture
Core ledger transactions must be strictly ACID compliant using PostgreSQL
Message brokering for transactional alerts and AML checks must use Apache Kafka
System must comply with PCI-DSS regarding any card data storage

operating_environment.frontend: Next.js applications for both Customer portal and Admin dashboard, securely served via HTTPS.
operating_environment.backend: Spring Boot microservices containerized via Docker and orchestrated in Kubernetes.

FR-01.title: Authentication & Multi-Factor Security
requirements: Users must log in with a username/email and a strong password
MFA is mandatory for login; configurable via SMS, Email, or TOTP (Google Authenticator)
An automatic session timeout triggers after 10 minutes of inactivity
After 5 failed login attempts, the account is temporarily locked for 30 minutes

FR-02.title: KYC & Identity Verification
requirements: New registrants must upload a government-issued ID and a live selfie video
System must integrate with a third-party API (e.g., Onfido) to calculate a confidence score
Compliance Officers are alerted for manual review if the automated confidence score falls below 85%
Accounts remain in a 'restricted' state (no outbound transfers) until KYC is verified

FR-03.title: Account Creation & Ledger Management
requirements: Customers can open Checking and Savings accounts natively in the app
Each account is assigned a unique system-generated Account Number and Routing Number
The core ledger must support double-entry accounting where every debit is paired with a credit
Accounts must display real-time 'Available Balance' distinct from 'Current Balance'

FR-04.title: Dashboard & Financial Overview
requirements: The main screen aggregates total net worth based on all active accounts
Displays rapid-action buttons: 'Transfer', 'Pay Bill', 'Send Money'
Provides a visual chart (bar/pie) of monthly expenses vs. income
Shows the top 5 most recent transactions globally

FR-05.title: Internal Fund Transfers
requirements: Customers can instantly transfer funds between their own accounts (Checking to Savings, etc.)
Transfers must execute immediately with zero fees
Transaction records are written to both accounts synchronously

FR-06.title: Domestic Fund Transfers (ACH/RTGS)
requirements: Users can send funds to any domestic bank using an Account Number and Routing Number
Users choose speed: 'Standard' (1-2 days) or 'Instant' (utilizing instant payment networks)
System validates daily transfer limits based on the user's KYC tier
MFA challenge is required before executing any domestic transfer exceeding $1,000

FR-07.title: International Wire Transfers (SWIFT)
requirements: Users can execute cross-border transfers specifying a SWIFT/BIC code and IBAN
System must fetch and display real-time foreign exchange (FX) rates before confirmation
Clear breakdown of intermediary bank fees must be shown
International transfers are routed to the AML validation queue via Kafka before execution

FR-08.title: Transaction History & Statements
requirements: Infinite-scroll list of all historical transactions grouped by month
Search functionality allowing text-match on merchant name or reference note
Filters available for date range, transaction type, and minimum/maximum amounts
Users can export specific date-range statements as digitally signed PDF or CSV files

FR-09.title: Beneficiary Management & Payees
requirements: Users can permanently save recipient details as 'Saved Beneficiaries'
Beneficiary records mandate a name, routing/SWIFT code, and account number
Adding a new beneficiary requires an OTP verification flow to prevent account takeover fraud

FR-10.title: Bill Payments & Recurring Schedules
requirements: Integration with regional biller networks allowing users to lookup providers (Utility, Telecom)
Users can execute one-time bill payments or set a recurring schedule (e.g., '15th of every month')
If the configured account lacks sufficient funds 2 days before a recurring payment, an alert is sent

FR-11.title: Physical & Virtual Card Management
requirements: Users can view their issued physical debit/credit cards linked to their accounts
Users can instantly freeze/unfreeze a card or report it lost/stolen
Users can generate single-use virtual cards for secure online shopping
Card details (CVV, full PAN) are masked by default and require biometric/password interaction to view

FR-12.title: Fixed Deposits (Term Deposits)
requirements: Users can open a fixed deposit by moving funds out of checking
A calculator dynamically shows the projected maturity amount based on term length (3, 6, 12 months) and APR
Early withdrawal incurs an automated penalty fee calculated against accrued interest

FR-13.title: Loan Application & Origination
requirements: Users can apply for personal loans specifying amount and term length
System triggers an external API call to pull a credit score
Automated decision engine calculates maximum eligibility based on internal history and credit score
Accepted loans automatically disburse funds to the user's primary checking account

FR-14.title: Notifications & Alerts Engine
requirements: Users configure their alert preferences (Push, SMS, Email)
Real-time alerts triggered upon profile changes, large transactions, or failed login attempts
Kafka topics are used asynchronously to dispatch alerts without blocking the main thread

FR-15.title: Customer Service Messaging & Disputes
requirements: Users can initiate a secure chat session with a bank representative
Users can flag a specific transaction from their history and formally open a 'Dispute'
Disputes require an uploaded attachment (e.g., receipt screenshot) and description

FR-16.title: Admin Dashboard & AML/Fraud Monitoring
requirements: Compliance officers view an inbox of transactions flagged by the automated risk engine
Officers can accept or reject transactions, releasing or returning funds respectively
Admins can force-lock customer accounts and require branch visits for high-severity violations
All admin actions write directly to a secure Audit Log table

NFR-01.title: Security and Data Integrity
requirements: All PII and passwords must be encrypted using AES-256 at rest
MFA tokens and OTPs expire exactly 5 minutes after generation
API payloads must implement symmetric encryption for all fund transfer operations

NFR-02.title: Performance and Availability
requirements: Core ledger transactions must complete execution in under 200ms
System architecture must guarantee 99.99% uptime via multi-zone deployment models

system_attributes.consistency: Double-entry accounting ensures total debits always equal total credits. No race condition can bypass the transaction locks.
system_attributes.security: PCI-DSS compliance strictly achieved; full card numbers are never returned in flat API responses without tokenization.

FR-01.title: Implement Spring Security & JWT Service
FR-01.description: Configure Spring Security to handle username/password authentication. Build the JWT provider that generates access and refresh tokens. Add security filters to intercept all API routes.
acceptance_criteria: Tokens are signed with RS256 algorithm.
Endpoints returning 401 Unauthorized correctly state standard error messages.
Refresh token endpoint safely validates token age and user status.

FR-01.title: Build Multi-Factor Authentication (OTP/TOTP) Verification
FR-01.description: Develop the MFA service. If SMS is selected, integrate Twilio/AWS SNS to send 6-digit OTPs. If Authenticator is selected, generate a secret and verifiable QR code payload.
acceptance_criteria: OTP strings expire absolutely after 5 minutes.
A successful primary password login yields a temporary 'pre-auth' token required to submit the MFA code.

FR-01.title: Design and Build Login Frontend Flows
FR-01.description: Design the Next.js login screens including state transitions for standard credential input, MFA challenge overlay, and lockout warnings.
acceptance_criteria: Login page has no layout shift upon error rendering.
MFA input auto-focuses the first digit box and auto-advances on keystroke.

FR-02.title: Integrate Third-Party KYC Verification API
FR-02.description: Implement a connector service in Java to communicate with Onfido/Jumio. Parse webhook payload callbacks describing the verification success confidence and state.
acceptance_criteria: Callback webhook validates HMAC signatures to ensure origin authenticity.
If confidence is <85%, UserKYC entity status changes to 'PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW'.

FR-02.title: Build Onboarding Next.js Screens
FR-02.description: Create the onboarding pipeline connecting the user's demographic inputs with the KYC API's frontend SDK to capture camera images securely.
acceptance_criteria: Onboarding enforces sequential progression.
Camera permissions are requested gracefully with UX fallback if denied.

FR-03.title: Design Core Ledger Database Schema
FR-03.description: Design the PostgreSQL schema modeling Users, Accounts, and LedgerEntries. Ensure LedgerEntries have strong foreign keys, precision decimal columns, and an unmodifiable structure.
acceptance_criteria: Balances are derived from sum queries of LedgerEntries, not stored uniquely as a mutable float.
Table prevents updates/deletes using database-level triggers to guarantee immutability.

FR-03.title: Develop Account Creation Services
FR-03.description: Create backend endpoints allowing a fully KYC'd user to instantiate Checking or Savings accounts. Implement logic to generate universally unique 12-digit Account Numbers.
acceptance_criteria: A Luhn algorithm or similar checksum validates the generated Account Numbers internally.
Accounts default to $0 balance.

FR-04.title: Develop Aggregation Dashboard API
FR-04.description: Write a highly optimized GET /api/user/dashboard endpoint that queries all accounts owned by the user, summing available balances and grabbing the top 5 transactions without blocking.
acceptance_criteria: Response heavily caches non-real-time metrics via Redis.
Total net worth correctly parses cross-currency conversions if applicable.

FR-04.title: Design Web and Dashboard UI Elements
FR-04.description: In Next.js, use Chart.js or Recharts to visualize income vs expenses. Design the high-fidelity 'Quick Actions' horizontal banner.
acceptance_criteria: Charts are fully accessible and load dynamically avoiding SSR mismatches.
Loading skeleton states explicitly cover all independent fetching dashboard zones.

FR-05.title: Implement Internal Transfer Executer
FR-05.description: Build the core transactional method `executeInternalTransfer()`. It must perform a double-entry database transaction, debiting Account A and crediting Account B atomically.
acceptance_criteria: Database connection isolation level prevents dirty reads entirely (`ISOLATION_SERIALIZABLE`).
Attempting to transfer more than Available Balance throws `InsufficientFundsException` immediately rolling back.
Self-transfers to the identical account are gracefully rejected.

FR-06.title: Build Domestic ACH Integration Gateway
FR-06.description: Create a service layer communicating with a mock ACH/FedWire clearing house. Generate NACHA-formatted files (or API calls) for batch process domestic sends.
acceptance_criteria: Routing numbers validate explicitly against a cached geographic/bank identifier database.
Transaction initially creates a 'PENDING' LedgerEntry until clearing confirmation.

FR-06.title: MFA Challenge Interceptor for Expensive Transfers
FR-06.description: Write a backend interceptor logic where if a transaction > $1000 requires processing, the backend initially suspends the transaction and issues an MFA token challenge response back to the client.
acceptance_criteria: User receives request to input OTP. Endpoint accepts OTP and unfreezes the transaction.
State of the pending transaction is stored temporarily in Redis.

FR-07.title: Implement External FX Rate Service
FR-07.description: Use an external API provider (e.g., Fixer.io or Bloomberg) to pull real-time exchange rates caching them in Redis every 5 minutes to fuel the SWIFT interface.
acceptance_criteria: Rate calculations apply the Bank's predefined profit margin spread.
Rate is valid for exactly 60 seconds on the frontend.

FR-07.title: Kafka Producer for AML Processing Validation
FR-07.description: Modify the international transfer handler. Instead of immediate database insertion, it must parse an event and push it to an `aml_validation_queue` Kafka topic.
acceptance_criteria: Kafka events are structured robustly with schemas (Avro/Protobuf).
Application seamlessly pauses customer feedback to 'Processing Review'.

FR-08.title: Optimize Transaction Search and Filtering Queries
FR-08.description: Write optimized indexes and pagination logic in PostgreSQL. Allow combining ranges, specific string queries, and amount filters seamlessly on large multi-thousand row scopes.
acceptance_criteria: Queries use Keyset/Cursor pagination instead of OFFSET for high performance.
Text searching against merchant name is indexed properly.

FR-08.title: Generate PDF Account Statements
FR-08.description: Implement a reporting library (like JasperReports or iText) taking the result set of transactions and converting it into a branded PDF with the bank's letterhead and user details.
acceptance_criteria: PDF includes summary block (Starting bal, Total In, Total Out, Ending bal).
PDF enforces explicit Read-Only permissions directly at the metadata level.

FR-09.title: Implement Beneficiary Management Core Structure
FR-09.description: Create the REST endpoints for adding, fetching, and removing Beneficiaries. Attach the MFA challenge flow for the creation endpoint to mitigate takeover attacks.
acceptance_criteria: Beneficiary deletion simply soft-deletes leaving foreign keys intact globally for history.
Added beneficiaries appear as instant targets in the Transfers UI dropdown.

FR-10.title: Build Scheduler and Cron Processes for Payments
FR-10.description: Implement a reliable Quartz scheduler or Spring `@Scheduled` task to wake up daily, evaluate pending recurring utility and bill payments, and execute them safely.
acceptance_criteria: System properly isolates processed vs unprocessed to prevent duplicate firing.
Low balance scenarios emit distinct 'Warning' notifications immediately.

FR-11.title: Integrate Physical and Virtual Card Issuer APIs
FR-11.description: Interact with a card-issuing processor webhook (like Marqeta/Stripe). Handle logic issuing virtual PANs securely mapping directly back to the customer's checking ledger balance.
acceptance_criteria: Virtual cards are generated and immediately active with 0 deployment lag.
Freezing a card cascades an API call blocking external processor authorizations instantly.

FR-11.title: Develop Secure Card View UI Component
FR-11.description: Build the React component modeling a physical card. Require user to enter password before the UI fetches the CVV and complete PAN string.
acceptance_criteria: PAN input is completely masked on idle states.
React state clears card data dynamically after 30 seconds of display.

FR-12.title: Create Fixed Deposit Calculators and Executers
FR-12.description: Implement term length logic mapping specific Annual Percentage Yields (APY). Execute transfer to a locked savings vault on confirmation, generating the maturity date map.
acceptance_criteria: Principal amounts are safely segregated outside the 'available' checking pool.
Database correctly manages logic if the maturity date lands on a bank holiday.

FR-13.title: Build Credit Scoring Interface
FR-13.description: Design an automated underwriting criteria processor requesting score from Experian/Equifax APIs, factoring their internal deposit history heavily.
acceptance_criteria: External hits perform synchronous fallback handling in case Experian is down.
Decisions format dynamically returning reasoning mapping (e.g. 'Debt to income ratio exceeded').

FR-14.title: Implement Notification Dispatcher via Kafka
FR-14.description: Setup a microservice that listens to 'transaction_events' on Kafka. Resolves customer notification preferences, routing logic outbound via AWS SES for Email or Twilio for SMS.
acceptance_criteria: SMS deliveries are correctly batched where required to maintain low throughput cost.
Consumers scale out automatically depending on the kafka partition lag.

FR-15.title: Develop Dispute Processing Module
FR-15.description: Enhance transaction history interface enabling users to flag an item. Develop the schema and API to persist case narratives and ingest supporting file blobs.
acceptance_criteria: Flagged transactions explicitly reflect 'Disputed' visual statuses natively.
Case opens dynamically in the Customer Support administrative board.

FR-16.title: Design Compliance Dashboard and Audit Trail UX
FR-16.description: Create the Admin Portal targeting Compliance Officers. Develop data-tables to review flagged AML transactions and explicitly define accept/reject controls.
acceptance_criteria: Suspicious metrics surface strictly prioritizing urgency variables.
Audit logs clearly show exactly which Compliance Officer approved a specific payload.

FR-16.title: Write Compliance Rules Engine logic
FR-16.description: Implement threshold detection configurations checking volume, source/destination match-ups against global OFAC sanctions lists natively blocking matches from resolving.
acceptance_criteria: Rules engine analyzes incoming SWIFT transfers natively resolving exact text matching and string distance measurements.
